name: Quest Marker
description: A UI marker that points players toward an objective, point of interest, or quest-relevant target.
design_notes: Quest markers guide attention, but they can also flatten exploration. The design should decide whether markers show exact positions, approximate areas, distance, verticality, objective state, and whether players can disable or filter them.
design_purpose: Help players locate active objectives without searching menus repeatedly.
Communicate objective distance, direction, and completion state.
Reduce navigation friction while preserving discovery where desired.
player_fantasy: The player feels oriented toward their chosen goal without losing track of the world.

parameters.name: marker_screen_edge_padding
parameters.description: Padding used when clamping offscreen markers to screen edges.

parameters.name: objective_area_radius
parameters.description: Approximate search area shown instead of exact point.

parameters.name: distance_label_threshold
parameters.description: Distance range where numeric labels should be shown.

parameters.name: marker_priority_weight
parameters.description: Ordering weight when multiple quest markers compete for attention.

edge_cases: Objectives inside caves, buildings, or stacked floors need vertical and pathing cues, not only straight-line direction.
Multiple active quests can flood the HUD unless filtered or prioritized.
Moving objectives and NPCs need marker smoothing without lying about position.
Markers should handle hidden, failed, completed, and optional objectives distinctly.
common_bugs: Marker points to world origin after the quest target despawns.
Straight-line distance sends players toward cliffs or locked doors without context.
Completed markers remain on compass because quest state and marker state update in different systems.
Offscreen clamping overlaps subtitles or critical combat UI.
balancing_notes: Exact markers reduce friction but can remove exploration and environmental reading.
Area markers preserve mystery while still giving players enough direction to proceed.
accessibility_notes: Use shape, text, and distance labels in addition to color, and provide marker size/opacity settings.
Allow players to reduce marker clutter or pin only one objective at a time.

implementation_notes.unity: Bind markers to objective_id plus target entity, world position, or objective area. Project world-to-screen after camera update and remove markers when quest state changes to completed, failed, or hidden.
implementation_notes.godot: Store marker state as objective_id, target NodePath or area center, and marker mode. Update screen-edge clamps after camera transforms and handle above/below indicators from world height delta.
implementation_notes.unreal: Feed UMG marker widgets from a quest tracker component that owns objective IDs, target actors, and area radii. Use weak actor references and clear widgets when replicated quest state changes.
implementation_notes.web: Keep quest markers in normalized state keyed by objective_id. Resolve target position each frame, clamp offscreen markers with padding, and hide stale markers when target or quest state is invalid.

example_games.title: The Elder Scrolls V: Skyrim
example_games.note: Uses compass and map quest markers to guide objectives.

example_games.title: The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt
example_games.note: Uses tracked quest markers and minimap routing for objectives.
